Building 1 is operational, based on there being minimal construction work and cars in the main carpark. Building 2 still has some construction work around the building. Building 15 roof is complete. The on-site power at Socrates South and Socrates North has some outlines of gas turbines, to be installed later.

Buildings 2-6 are operational, based on all equipment being in place, the paved ground, and minimal construction equipment by the buildings. Buildings 7-11 all have some kind of power or cooling equipment lining their right side which are not fully installed. Building 13 has chillers installed. Buildings 15-19 roofs are complete. The Socrates South power plant to the right of Buildings 7-11 has 7 gas turbines in place, judging by the exhaust stacks.
Building 12 is [operational](https://vantage-dc.com/data-center-locations/north-america/new-albany-ohio)

The Socrates South power plant to the right of Buildings 7-11 now has 10 natural gas turbines in place, judging by the number of exhaust stacks. Battery backup has been installed nearby. To the north, a new section of tent buildings has begun east of Buildings 15-19. It is unclear how those buildings will be powered, if we assume that the Socrates North power plant is just powering Buildings 15-19.

Building 7-11 (Meta tents) are operational. In January we saw them look almost complete, with some work still ongoing for power equipment. Assuming 3 more months for commissioning, we expect operation by May.
Building 13 (Vantage) is operational, based on it starting construction about 3 months after Building 12.
Building 14 (Vantage) operational, based on it starting construction about 3 months after Building 13.

Socrates South and Socrates North gas power plants are operational, coinciding with Building 15-19 being operational. Based [on](https://dis.puc.state.oh.us/CaseRecord.aspx?CaseNo=25-0188-EL-BGN) [documents](https://dis.puc.state.oh.us/ViewImage.aspx?CMID=A1001001A25E29B42006E01696) [stating](https://www.williams.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/8/2025/03/Socrates-North-Fact-Sheet-Final-1.pdf) "late Third or early Fourth Quarter of 2026", "late 2026", "November 2026", "third quarter of 2026".
Land clearing begins at site 2 (northeast). Buildings 1 and 2 in site 1 (northwest) are not running AI workloads at this point as is evident from the lack of cooling infrastructure.
Land cleared for site 1 expansion, some materials down at substation site. Building 1 retrofitted and likely operational.

Building 2 (second square building) retrofitted with cooling infrastructure. Building 5 roof complete. Buildings 3 and 4 appear operational. Land cleared for site 3 (south). More activity around substation area.
Building 6 roof complete and Building 5 now has chillers so is likely operational. Land clearing is complete and construction has begun at site 3.
Building 7 now has chillers and Site 1 appears completely operational. Buildings 8 and 9 are partially built.
Building 10 roof is complete but neither it, nor Building 8/9 have complete cooling and generating equipment yet.
Second part of substation seems to be complete. Building 9 also appears complete, probably enabled by the additional power supply.

Cooling equipment seems to have been installed on Buildings 8 and 10 as well as Building 11. Building 12 is structurally complete but the chillers and generators aren't finished. Buildings 13 and 14 are partially built.
Site 2 appears fully operational. Buildings 12 and 14 appear complete as well.
Building 15 looks structurally complete.
Site 3 fully operational.

timeline.status: Land clearing begins next to the existing building.

Battery facility connected to the building. 121 air-cooled chillers installed, which could support 200 MW. In the Duke faciltiy in nearby Mississippi, several natural gas turbines are in place but not fully connected up.

Battery facility looks complete (though more might be added). Turbines look connected up, minimal construction around them. Based on this, and on [earlier](https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1947715674429919279) [tweets](https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1950251710141567110) from Elon Musk, 110,000 NVIDIA GB200 GPUs are operational.

Since October, an array of dry cooling air-cooled condensers has been added (the array of large fans to the left of the building), and appears almost complete. Two more similar arrays appear to be under construction. More air-cooled chillers, batteries, and gas turbines have also been installed.

The facility is at the full ~1 gigawatt, 550,000 GPU capacity that was planned as of mid-2025. The timing is based on the air-cooled condensers needing to be complete to handle this power capacity. On 2026-01-13, the third (presumed to be final) array of condensers was roughly at the stage that the first array of condensers was 3 months prior, in October. Based on that, we estimate it will be completed 3 months later, in April 2026. Other components can be completed in parallel to that, but we estimate about a month more will still be needed to put the finishing touches on the data center. That brings the estimated timing to May 2026.
Construction starts with a road added on site, retrofitting a former Electrolux building.
First [significant/useful training workload](https://x.ai/colossus) on 25k H100s.
[100k H100s online](https://archive.is/z39uN).
150k H100s and 50k H200s online. This is roughly when Musk said, plus on this date they reported "running jobs with 150K+ GPUs".
Second substation almost connected (red cables)
30k B200s added (in GB200 configuration).
Third substation looks completed, but it doesn't appear to be connected to the building yet. Some air-cooled chillers removed, while large cooling towers are added.
There is still construction visible in the third substation yard and next to the new cooling towers.
Third substation was expected to power the building in Fall.

All air-cooled chillers have been disconnected and/or removed from the site, leaving only the cooling towers for cooling. The third substation looks complete, with red cables connected to the building.

timeline.status: Land clearing begins for northern two buildings (Building 1-2)

Building 1 looks close to finishing construction, with chillers in place. OpenAI [stated](https://openai.com/index/five-new-stargate-sites/) that "Oracle began delivering the first NVIDIA GB200 racks in June." The "tenant improvement" work [is not due](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025000156) until September, however.

Building 1 is fully operational around this date. The first phase was "live" on September 30 according a Crusoe [announcement](https://www.crusoe.ai/resources/newsroom/crusoe-announces-flagship-abilene-data-center-is-live). While the first phase consists of Building 1 and 2, the language doesn't outright confirm that both buildings are fully operational. The announcement also stated that "Oracle began delivering the first NVIDIA GB200 racks in June", and we think 3 months is enough time to ram to 50,000 GB200s in Building 1. The satellite image also suggests that Building 1 is complete (shell and equipment done, many cars neatly parked) while Building 2 is ongoing (trucks and construction equipment still present). The 200 MW [substation](https://www.mortenson.com/projects/abilene-data-center-development) built in 2022 and expanded in 2024 is enough to power both buildings. To the right of Building 2, five gas turbines are in place with five more turbines still under construction. Chillers are partly installed for Building 3. Roof is complete for Building 4. The larger 1 GW substation is not complete yet.

Building 2 is fully operational. This exact date corresponds to the expected completion date of "Four (4) data hall spaces and an administrative office build-out" according to a [TDLR filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025007742), but this date is also consistent with satellite imagery. By January 15, Building 2 looked as finished as Building 1, whereas on September 26 it did not look finished.

The 1 GW substation looks close to completion. Building 3 appears to have all power and cooling equipment installed, but there are still signs of construction work. This seems on track for operation in 3 months. Building 4 has about 80% of chillers installed. Other buildings have few or no chillers installed.

Building 3 and 4 look complete, but are not fully operational. Not as many cars are parked next to it as Building 1-2 which suggests they may still be ramping up. Building 4 was due by now according to a [regulatory filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025014756). Building 5 still has light construction activity, while Building 6 still lacks chillers. The on-site power plant to the right of Building 2 looks complete.

Buildings 3 and 4 are estimated to be operational. On April 22nd, Oracle [posted](https://x.com/i/status/2047012589302153558) that "In Abilene, 200MW is already operational". This suggests that there was still only Building 1 and 2 operational. Since Buildings 3 and 4 looked complete from the outside on March 24th, we give it another month after Oracle's post for them to be operational. This would also give the buildings a similar 1 year 4 months timeline that Buildings 1 took from land clearing to being operational.

Buildings 5-8 are estimated to be operational. On March 24, Building 8 roughly looked like where Building 3 was in September. Building 3 is estimated to be operational in April, 7 months later. This naively suggests Building 8 will be operational by early November, 7 months after March 24.

timeline.status: Land clearing begins for east buildings

Building 1 is operational. Google Earth shows Building 1 had all backup generators and cooling towers installed by the end of August, but construction work was ongoing and there were almost no cars parked. By February 2025, Google Earth shows no construction work and many cars. So our best guess is in the middle, in November 2024. Building 3 roof is complete.
Building 2 is operational. Assuming it takes 3 months after generators and cooling towers are finished, similar to Building 1.
Land clearing begins in the southwest, suggesting more buildings are planned.

Building 3 has all 34 backup generators installed and 4 cooling towers installed, with 3 more cooling towers under construction. There are gaps between cooling towers suggesting that more could be added. Buildings 1 and 2 are running close to full capacity, given that water vapor is coming out of 5 of 7 cooling towers for each building. The land cleared in the southwest appears to be shaped for two more similar buildings, with notches for substations.
Building 3 is operational. Assuming it takes 2 months to install all 7 cooling towers, then another 3 months for final touches and commissioning.

Building 1, which was first operational in 2021, has increased power capacity by about 75%. This is based on the expansion of the dry cooling array from 4 to 7 columns (seen on [Google Earth](https://earth.google.com/web/@39.4948785,-119.43174359,1568.8659293a,562.69639351d,35y,-0h,0t,0r/)). Its backup generators also grew from 18 to 33, an 83% increase. Around this time (2023 Q3) the quarterly PUE of the campus is 1.24, down from the initial 1.29 in 2022 Q3. Since no other buildings have been added, these changes indicate an increase in power density, which may entail a new generation of hardware getting installed.
Building 2 now has generators in place, but there is still a crane and construction work present.

Building 2 is operational. Based on a [Google Earth image](https://earth.google.com/web/@39.49733738,-119.43113543,1552.05877612a,1467.7067385d,35y,0h,0t,0r/) showing complete building shell, mechanical equipment and generators, plus cars parked at the front.
Building 3 foundation is underway. Building 2 exterior looks totally clean/landscaped.
Building 3 has a partially complete shell, electrical and mechanical equipment. Building 4 foundation is underway.

Building 3 is estimated to be operational. We estimate the operational date by analogy to the time that Building 1 took to go from a foundation to being operational. The power capacity is based on the number of generators reported in an air quality permit [application](https://ndep-onbase.nv.gov/api/Document/AeFo%C3%89pjclbkomJ7CWpPryLOjA6FQT9HFrkSuMWbMXTpKCzSR%C3%81W1f8zzFhGhik1iZCcNSpuTRRqNXD1inrrpBxtA%3D/) (p.92-93). The dry cooler array is more than double the size of the Building 1 array, which suggests an even larger power capacity than we estimate, but we believe the generator-based estimate is more reliable here.
Land clearing begins for Building 1, also known as CB-1, the first AI/HPC building on the site.

Building 1 is operational. Based on Terawulf [saying](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/126/terawulf-reports-third-quarter-2025-results) "As of September 30, 2025, the site has energized 245 MW of Bitcoin-mining capacity and 22.5 MW of HPC capacity." The small "Wulf Den" building was [said](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/99/terawulf-to-deliver-over-70-mw-of-data-center) to be 2 MW of IT power while CB-1 would be 16 MW. This is roughly consistent with 22.5 MW of total facility power. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/99/terawulf-to-deliver-over-70-mw-of-data-center) to Core42, a subsidiary of UAE-based G42. It likely uses NVIDIA DGX B200 servers.

Buildings 1-3 all appear to have a complete building and cooling equipment. Building 4 shell is still under construction, but 17 cooling units are already loaded on the roof. The outline of Building 5 is visible at the foundation.

Building 2 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB2A is operational, presumably making up half the final capacity. CB2B was expected in March 2026. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/99/terawulf-to-deliver-over-70-mw-of-data-center) to Core42, a subsidiary of UAE-based G42. It likely uses NVIDIA DGX B200 servers.

Building 3 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB3 was expected to be energized by mid-May 2026. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/112/terawulf-signs-200-mw-10-year-ai-hosting-agreements-with) to Fluidstack, likely contains [Google TPUs](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-offers-its-tpus-to-ai-cloud-providers-report/), and is likely used by [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-invests-50-billion-in-american-ai-infrastructure).

Building 4 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB4 was expected to be energized by Q3 2026. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/112/terawulf-signs-200-mw-10-year-ai-hosting-agreements-with) to Fluidstack, likely contains [Google TPUs](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-offers-its-tpus-to-ai-cloud-providers-report/), and is likely used by [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-invests-50-billion-in-american-ai-infrastructure).

Building 5 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB5 was expected to be energized by Q4 2026. However, based on the pace of Building 4, and comparisons to other projects like Stargate Abilene, we think this is optimistic. We expect a 3-month delay past the end of Q4 2026 to get to full operational capacity. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/112/terawulf-signs-200-mw-10-year-ai-hosting-agreements-with) to Fluidstack, likely contains [Google TPUs](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-offers-its-tpus-to-ai-cloud-providers-report/), and is likely used by [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-invests-50-billion-in-american-ai-infrastructure).

Estimate: All 6 AI data center buildings will be finished in 2028, assuming a groundbreak-to-operational timeline of 18-24 months. One is complete, three are clearly under construction, and the last two data centers are still breaking ground and laying foundations.
Construction beginning, as can be seen on satellite. Major renovations on western side of the non AI building (PX1), including adding generators and cooling towers.
Major construction has finished on AI building 1, and AI building 6 has been expedited, as depicted by satellite imagery.

Estimated: AI building 1 should be operational. Major construction work finished by January 31, 2026, and the shell looked complete by that date. We estimate after three months the building will be operational.

Although official [promotional sources](https://www.streamdatacenters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/SDC-Goodyear-CaseStudy-251218.pdf) estimate the campus to be fully operational in 10-12 years, it is likely to be finished sooner than this given the empirical durations for other data center campuses as well as because AI building 6 has been expedited. The first AI building took approximately 24 months to complete. Assuming construction for a new building starts midway for every building, then the campus will be complete in 4 years for the 5 remaining buildings, since AI buildings 2 and 6 are both underway.

timeline.status: Land clearing begins for Building 1 (SIN01)

Building 1 (SIN01) is completed, but at this point it likely not being used for AI as the collaboration with Microsoft that resulted in GB300s did not occur until Q1 2026. The completion is based both on satellite imagery, which shows the building fully completed, and a [March 2025 article](https://econews.pt/2025/03/24/start-campus-to-start-construction-of-second-data-centre-building-in-sines-by-end-june/) that says it "has been in operation since October", which is October of 2024.

Building 1 (SIN01) operational for AI, with the first deployment of 12,600 Nvidia GB300 NVL72s to the site being reported completed "Q1 2026" based on the [corporate announcement](https://lp.startcampus.pt/start-campus-hosts-eus-first-nvidia-gb300-chips-with-nscale-for-microsoft). Land clearing also begins for Building 2 (SIN02), with faint dirt outlines for roads and building foundations visible.

Building 2 (SIN02) projected to be operational. This is based on the timeline provided in the [factsheet](https://23712035.hs-sites.com/hubfs/SINES_02.PDF.pdf?hsCtaAttrib=199407154715) for the site. We expect VR200 GPUs to be used as a lot of those chips will have already been available in September 2027, and the "up to 600kW racks" in the factsheet suggest they are aiming for this.
Building 1 is operational, based on default roof-to-operational time.
Building 2 is being retrofitted from a warehouse. The roof is being painted white, and cooling towers and a new substation are being built just south of the building (based on later imagery).
The new substation for Building 2 looks mostly complete, half of the cooling towers are installed. The black patch in the top left of the building suggests that the new roof is not quite complete.

Building 2 is operational. It took about 6 months to build the first set of cooling towers and the second set was just starting in October, so that should take until around April 2026. The new roof looked on track to be completed in December and by default we assume operation 5 months after that (i.e. also April 2026). But we estimate that previous buildings on this campus took a month or two longer, so our best estimate is May 2026.

timeline.status: Land clearing begins.

Foundations are underway for the main buildings, and the overall layout aligns with the [architectural renderings](https://engineering.fb.com/2025/09/29/data-infrastructure/metas-infrastructure-evolution-and-the-advent-of-ai/) published in 2025.

New [power stations](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1D4eUHwEuirNi0g3IU_xGOti3PcC8fcXi/view?usp=sharing) are in service for a 1.8 GW load at Holly Ridge. However, the data center is still not operational.

First phase is operational, based on a [Meta blog post](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1PmFNgOwkPepwjpGIbNUuPJP0LNgIqe7l/view?usp=sharing). We assume that this aligns with the three natural gas turbines being completed, for a total of 2262 MW capacity, but that these turbines have a backup role. This is close to the 1800 MW of load requested from the grid. Meta [claims](https://engineering.fb.com/2025/09/29/data-infrastructure/metas-infrastructure-evolution-and-the-advent-of-ai/) that "The Hyperion cluster will have a 5-gigawatt capacity once complete", but so far there is no concrete evidence for this happening at the Holly Ridge site alone.
Land clearing begins
Building 1 and 2 under construction.

Construction on Building 1 and 2 looks about halfway to completion. Foundations and utility trenches are visible for Building 3-4. Building 5-6 have an outline. The substation appears to be expanding.

Building 1 operational. First two buildings are [expected to complete construction by Spring 2026](https://web.archive.org/web/20250923144616/https://www.facebook.com/plugins/post.php?href=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2Farmdems%2Fposts%2Fupdate-on-lima-road-and-the-data-center-from-armstrong-county-democratic-chair-s%2F122148303506797709%2F). We use the midpoint of Spring (2026-04-15) as a best guess for completion. But this is when the buildings are "turned over to the tenant", so it might take more time to install the IT equipment and commission the data center - we think about 2 months. So we estimate 2026-06-15. This [regulatory document](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025027064) lists the completion date for Building 1 as 2026-09-12 but this is probably conservative.

Building 2 operational. The completion date for [Building 2 Tenant Improvement](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025027065) is 2026-11-07, about 2 months later than Building 1. We therefore add 2 months to the Building 1 operational date.

6 buildings are operational. This is based on:
1. A [regulatory filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2026010861) for Building 6 with a completion date of 2027-07-30. Normally there is a follow-up filing for "tenant improvement" which involves building the interior, with a completion date 1-3 months after the initial filing.
2. A project deadline of EOY 2027 in the [original tax abatement agreement](https://www.co.armstrong.tx.us/upload/page/5361/Phase%201%20Tax%20Abatement%20Agreement%20-%20Signed.pdf)
3. The similar 8-building project in Abilene taking an estimated 2 years and 2 months.
Land clearing begins
Roof complete on Buildings 3
Buildings 1-4 are operational. Buildings 1-2 had roofs as of 2025-09-21 are likely operational in 5 months, but buildings 3-4 appear about 3 months behind them, suggesting an 8-month total duration.

Buildings 1-14 operational. Buildings 1-4 will likely take over 2 years on this site. By comparison, Buildings 1-7 at the New Carlisle site were completed in less than 1.5 years. Assuming a similar pace, we expect 10 additional buildings to take about 2 years after 2025-09-21. This aligns with [expected completion by 2027](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/aws-confirmed-as-company-behind-10bn-mississippi-data-center-development/) and a [permit review document](https://opc.deq.state.ms.us/get_doc.aspx?dt=eparat&id=1747289) from 2025-06-25 for this site, stating the project will take “years” to build.

timeline.status: Land clearing begins for Building 1 and 2
Building 1 and 2 are partly constructed. No clear evidence of more buildings starting construction.

Building 1 and 2 are operational. The similar data center in Ajman, UAE was delayed by over a year, now due in December 2026 (compare [1](https://khaznadatacenters.com/press-release/khazna-expands-infrastructure-to-accelerate-uaes-leadership-in-artificial-intelligence/), [2](https://khaznadatacenters.com/press-release/khazna-announces-the-uaes-first-ai-optimized-data-center-and-the-largest-in-its-portfolio-powering-the-nations-drive-toward-ai-leadership/)). The buildings here looked 3-6 months behind the Ajman building as of November 2025, but they were moving faster. So we think Building 1 and 2 are on track for 200 MW by December 2026, along with the Ajman building.

1 GW of IT power is operational. We haven’t seen expansion beyond 200 MW as of November 2025. Optimistically, 8 more 100 MW buildings start construction in December 2025, in parallel, and take 1.5 years like the first two buildings, hitting 1 GW by Q3 2027. More realistically, there's some staggering of construction over several months, like Stargate Abilene. Our median estimate is January 2028.
